ogTitle: Brooms Head vs Broughams Gate: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Brooms Head and Broughams Gate.
heading: Brooms Head vs Broughams Gate — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Brooms Head, New South Wales and Broughams Gate, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Broughams Gate has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Brooms Head (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Brooms Head or Broughams Gate safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Brooms Head has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Broughams Gate scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Brooms Head scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Broughams Gate scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Brooms Head or Broughams Gate?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Broughams Gate has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
